# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  protect_from_forgery with: :exception

  # POST /images/convert
  # params:
  #   image: uploaded file
  #   format: target format (e.g. "png", "jpg")
  #   size: resize geometry (e.g. "800x800", "800x800>", "50%")
  def convert
    unless params[:image].respond_to?(:path)
      render json: { error: "missing image" }, status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    input_path = params[:image].path
    format = params[:format].to_s
    size = params[:size].to_s

    out_dir = Rails.root.join("tmp", "processed")
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(out_dir)

    out_name = "#{SecureRandom.hex(12)}.#{format}"
    output_path = out_dir.join(out_name).to_s

    cmd = "convert #{input_path} -resize #{size} #{output_path}"
    system(cmd)

    if File.exist?(output_path)
      send_file output_path, disposition: "inline", type: "image/#{format}"
    else
      render json: { error: "conversion failed", cmd: cmd }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end
end
